summary: The average time to respond to a vital record request fell sharply through fiscal 2024 (to 6.9 days) as DORIS's online Historical Vital Records platform let most customers get copies without submitting a staff-processed request. It then rose again in fiscal 2025 (to 12.9 days) after a staff member in the vital-records unit was temporarily moved to cover an administrative vacancy; DORIS expected the reassigned worker to return in fiscal 2026, and response time did improve back to 10.7 days that year.
oneLiner: decreasing overall, despite a temporary rise from a staff reassignment
